rules.instruction: Tests must call the production function being tested.
Never use self.assertTrue(True), skippable conditional assertions, or tests that only verify callable().
Construct dataclasses using their actual fields and valid combinations.
When behavior depends on status, mock downstream calls and verify whether they were or were not called.
Do not remove or overwrite existing tests unless explicitly required.
Add one focused test per requested behavior and avoid unrelated test refactoring.
When importing a production class whose name begins with Test into a pytest test module, alias it to a name that does not begin with Test to avoid collection warnings.
When a required function argument is added, search all existing calls and update every fixture, including older control-flow tests.
For status-driven code, verify both the returned status and the observable control flow, such as whether downstream functions were called.
Prefer logically consistent test data. Do not combine passed=True with a failure status unless the test explicitly verifies field copying without validation.
When testing exceptions, assert the specific exception type raised by the production code rather than a broader or assumed exception.
